# Xtate.Core — Copilot Agent Instructions

## What This Repository Is

**Xtate.Core** is the core library of the [Xtate](https://xtate.net/) state-machine framework.
It implements the [W3C SCXML specification](https://www.w3.org/TR/scxml/) in C#, providing:

- An SCXML interpreter (`StateMachineInterpreter`)
- A fluent C# builder for state machines (`StateMachineFluentBuilder`)
- An IoC container integration layer built on `Xtate.IoC`
- Persistence, external services, I/O processors (HTTP, Named Pipe, SCXML)
- Data model abstractions and an XPath data model implementation
- Resource loaders, actions, logging, and options infrastructure

**License:** AGPL-3.0-or-later  
**Author:** Sergii Artemenko / T.S.S.Art  
**NuGet package:** `Xtate.Core`

---

## Repository Layout

```
/
├── src/Xtate.Core/          # Main library (single project)
│   ├── Actions/             # Custom action infrastructure (System: Start/Destroy)
│   ├── Class/               # StateMachineClass hierarchy (Runtime/Location/Scxml variants)
│   ├── Common/              # Shared utilities: extensions, logging, polyfills, TaskMonitor
│   ├── DataModel/           # Data model abstraction, evaluators, handler base
│   ├── DataTypes/           # DataModelValue, DataModelList, DataModelNumber, DataModelDateTime
│   ├── ExternalServices/    # External service infrastructure (HTTP, SMTP clients)
│   ├── Http/                # HTTP IoProcessor support
│   ├── Interpreter/         # Core SCXML interpreter (StateMachineInterpreter)
│   ├── IoC/                 # IoC extensions: AncestorTracker, Options, ServiceArray, Tools, TransformArgs
│   ├── IoProcessors/        # I/O processor abstractions, HTTP and Named Pipe processors
│   ├── Persistence/         # State persistence abstractions and storage layer
│   ├── Properties/          # GlobalUsings.cs, Resources.resx
│   ├── ResourceLoaders/     # SCXML resource loading (resx, file, HTTP)
│   ├── Scxml/               # SCXML serializer/deserializer, XInclude support
│   ├── StateMachine/        # SCXML model interfaces (IState, ITransition, etc.) and model builder
│   ├── StateMachineFluentBuilder/ # Fluent C# API for building state machines
│   ├── StateMachineHost/    # Host-level services: scope management, event scheduling, security
│   └── StateMachineOptions/ # StateMachineOptions class
│
├── test/Xtate.Core.Test/    # MSTest test project
│   ├── UnitTests/           # Unit tests (FinalStateTest, JsonSerializationTest, …)
│   ├── HostedTests/         # Integration tests using the full host + SCXML files
│   ├── Interpreter/         # Interpreter-level tests
│   ├── Scxml/               # SCXML parsing/serialization tests
│   ├── DI/                  # DI container tests
│   └── DevTests/            # Developer/experimental tests
│
├── Directory.Build.props    # Autogenerated; sets LangVersion=preview, Nullable=enable, analyzers
├── Directory.Packages.props # Central package versioning (Xtate.IoC, System.Threading.Channels)
├── Global.Packages.props    # (if present) Additional global package overrides
└── .github/workflows/       # CI: publish.yml (build + test + NuGet publish on tag)
```

---

## Build, Test, and Lint

### Prerequisites

- .NET 11 SDK (also builds for net10.0, net9.0, net8.0, netstandard2.0, net462)
- Mono (only needed for `net462` builds in CI)

### Commands

```bash
# Restore
dotnet restore

# Build (Debug)
dotnet build

# Build (Release)
dotnet build --configuration Release

# Run all tests
dotnet test

# Run tests (Release, no rebuild)
dotnet test --configuration Release --no-restore --no-build

# Pack NuGet package
dotnet pack --configuration Release -p:PackageVersion=<version> --include-symbols -p:SymbolPackageFormat=snupkg -o bin/packages
```

Code style is enforced by `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild=true` with analyzer rules—build warnings include style violations.

---

## Key Architectural Patterns

### IoC via `Xtate.IoC`

The project uses **Xtate.IoC** (not Microsoft DI). The API is similar but distinct:

```csharp
var services = new ServiceCollection();          // Xtate.IoC.ServiceCollection
services.AddModule<StateMachineProcessorModule>();
var provider = services.BuildProvider();         // BuildProvider(), not BuildServiceProvider()
var svc = await provider.GetRequiredService<IStateMachineScopeManager>();
```

Key differences from MS DI:
- `services.BuildProvider()` not `BuildServiceProvider()`
- Services use `[SetByIoC]` on `required` init-properties (constructor injection alternative)
- `[InstantiatedByIoC]` marks types that must be created by the container
- `SharedWithin.Container` / `SharedWithin.Scope` for lifetime control
- `Option.IfNotRegistered` for optional/fallback registrations
- `Services.AddForwarding<T>(...)` to forward one registration to another

### Module Pattern

All feature groups are registered through `Module` subclasses:

```csharp
// A module can declare dependencies on other modules as generic type args:
public class StateMachineProcessorModule
    : Module<ExternalServiceModule, EventSchedulerModule, StateMachineInterpreterModule, TaskMonitorModule>
{
    protected override void AddServices() { /* register services */ }
}
```

The primary entry-point module for running state machines is `StateMachineProcessorModule`.

### `required` Properties + `[SetByIoC]`

Constructor parameters are not used for DI. Instead, classes declare public `required` init-properties:

```csharp
public required IStateMachineCollection StateMachineCollection { private get; [SetByIoC] init; }
```

### `StateMachineClass` Hierarchy

To execute a state machine, create an instance of a `StateMachineClass` subclass:

| Class | Description |
|---|---|
| `RuntimeStateMachine` | Wraps an in-memory `IStateMachine` object |
| `LocationStateMachine` | Loads from a URI |
| `ScxmlStateMachine` | Wraps an `IScxmlStateMachine` |
| `ScxmlStringStateMachine` | Parses from a string |
| `ScxmlStreamStateMachine` | Parses from a `Stream` |

Then execute via `IStateMachineScopeManager`:

```csharp
var result = await scopeManager.Execute(new RuntimeStateMachine(stateMachine), SecurityContextType.NewStateMachine);
```

### `IServiceProvider` Alias

`IServiceProvider` is aliased to `Xtate.IoC.IServiceProvider` (not `System.IServiceProvider`).
`Empty` is aliased to `System.ValueTuple` (used as a unit/void type parameter).

---

## Namespace Conventions

Namespaces do **not** always match folder structure (editorconfig disables that rule project-wide).
The pattern is feature-area-based:

| Area | Namespace |
|---|---|
| Core types | `Xtate` |
| Interpreter | `Xtate.Interpreter`, `Xtate.Interpreter.Services`, `Xtate.Interpreter.Internal` |
| State machine model | `Xtate.StateMachine`, `Xtate.StateMachine.Builder.Services` |
| Data model | `Xtate.DataModel`, `Xtate.DataModel.Services` |
| Data types | `Xtate.DataTypes` |
| Host | `Xtate.StateMachineHost`, `Xtate.StateMachineHost.Services` |
| IoC extensions | `Xtate.IoC`, `Xtate.IoC.DependencyInjection`, `Xtate.IoC.AncestorTracker.*` |
| Logging | `Xtate.Logging`, `Xtate.Logging.Provider` |
| SCXML | `Xtate.Scxml` |
| Persistence | `Xtate.Persistence` |
| DI modules | `Xtate.<Area>.DependencyInjection` |
| Abstractions | `Xtate.<Area>.Abstractions` (rare; often just inline in parent namespace) |
| Class/entry points | `Xtate.Class` |

---

## Code Style

- **Indentation:** Tabs (width 4)
- **Nullable:** enabled everywhere (except `netstandard2.0`/`net462` targets)
- **Language version:** `preview` (latest C# features are used: primary constructors, field-backed properties via `field`, etc.)
- **`var` usage:** preferred in all cases
- **`this.` qualification:** not used
- **Using directives:** `System.*` sorted first; managed via `GlobalUsings.cs`
- **Modifier order:** `public, private, protected, internal, static, readonly, new, sealed, volatile, override, virtual, abstract, async`
- **ConfigureAwait:** All `await` calls use `.ConfigureAwait(false)` (enforced by `ConfigureAwaitChecker.Analyzer`)
- **File headers:** Every `.cs` file starts with the AGPL copyright block (see any existing file)
- **Region blocks:** `#region Interface <InterfaceName>` groups used to separate interface implementations
- **Access modifiers on init-properties:** `private get; [SetByIoC] init;` pattern on DI-injected props

---

## Testing

- **Framework:** MSTest (`Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting`)
- **Mocking:** Moq
- **Coverage:** Coverlet collector

Test classes are in `Xtate.Test` namespace. Key base class for integration tests: `HostedTestBase`.

Typical test setup:
```csharp
var services = new ServiceCollection();
services.AddModule<StateMachineProcessorModule>();
// add additional mocks/modules
var serviceProvider = services.BuildProvider();
var scopeManager = await serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IStateMachineScopeManager>();
```

Tests may use `[Parallelizable]`-like `[Parallelize]` attribute (custom `ParallelizeAttribute.cs`).

---

## CI / Publishing

- **Workflow:** `.github/workflows/publish.yml` triggers on version tags (`v*`) or manually
- **Versioning:** GitVersion (`gittools/actions/gitversion`) computes the full semver
- **Publishing targets:** NuGet.org and GitHub Packages
- **Secrets required:** `NUGET_ORG_TOKEN`, `XTATE_GITHUB_TOKEN`

There is no separate CI-only test workflow; tests run as part of the publish workflow.

---

## Common Errors and Workarounds

### `GetRequiredService` must be awaited
`provider.GetRequiredService<T>()` returns `ValueTask<T>` (not `T` directly). Always `await` it:
```csharp
var svc = await provider.GetRequiredService<IMyService>();
```

### `BuildProvider()` not `BuildServiceProvider()`
The Xtate IoC container uses `services.BuildProvider()`, not the MS DI `BuildServiceProvider()`.

### `IServiceProvider` conflict
`System.IServiceProvider` is shadowed by the global alias `IServiceProvider = Xtate.IoC.IServiceProvider`. If you need the BCL version, use the full name.

### `net462` / `netstandard2.0` builds
Nullable annotations are suppressed (`NoWarn nullable`) for these targets. Polyfill types are in `Common/Polyfills/`.

### SCXML resource loading in tests
Test projects embed SCXML files as resources (`EmbeddedResource`) and reference them via `resx://` URIs:
```csharp
var uri = new Uri($"resx://{assemblyName}/{assemblyName}/HostedTests/Scxml/{file}");
```
